title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: How to {{c1::reconcile the fact}}(调和这个事实) that they are {{c2::doing so well}}(表现得这么好) on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c3::pretty hard evals}}(评估过程相当困难).”
original: How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”
translation: 他们能在评估测试（evals）上表现得这么好，这怎么说得通呢？你看看这些评估，它们本身就非常难。
note:
phrases: reconcile the fact | doing so well | pretty hard evals
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0005_f794b9
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, {{c1::it turns out}}(结果发现). But also it’s {{c2::kind of abstract}}(比较抽象的概念).
original: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, it turns out. But also it’s kind of abstract.
translation: Ilya Sutskever 结果发现，我们适应新事物的速度非常快。但同时，它又有点抽象。
note:
phrases: it turns out | kind of abstract
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0002_2fe0f2
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: discourse_organizer | abstraction_bridge
transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.
expression_transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, {{c1::repeat itself twice}}(重复了两次) in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 但从经济影响来看，似乎还远远落后。这很难理解，一个模型怎么能一方面做出如此惊人的事情，另一方面又在某些情况下重复自己两次呢？
note:
phrases: repeat itself twice
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0005_f794b9
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: discourse_organizer | strong_collocation
transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.
expression_transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever Yeah. This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to {{c1::reconcile the fact}}(调和这个事实) that they are {{c2::doing so well}}(表现得这么好) on evals?
original: Ilya Sutskever Yeah. This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
translation: Ilya Sutskever 是的。这正是目前模型最让人困惑的事情之一。它们在评估测试上表现得这么好，这到底该如何调和呢？
note:
phrases: reconcile the fact | doing so well
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing {{c1::pre-training}}(预训练), the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer {{c2::was everything}}(就是一切).
original: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
translation: 但还有另一种解释。在人们进行预训练的时候，关于用什么数据来训练这个问题，其实就已经解决了，因为这个答案本身就是一切。
note:
phrases: pre-training | was everything
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source:

source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: abstraction_bridge | stance_positioning
transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: That’s all you see. It’s not really felt in any other way so far. Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Should we actually begin}}(我们真的该开始了没？) here?
original: That’s all you see. It’s not really felt in any other way so far. Dwarkesh Patel Should we actually begin here?
translation: 你看到的就这些。到目前为止，它并没有以其他任何方式被感受到。Dwarkesh Patel，我们是不是应该从这里开始呢？
note:
phrases: Should we actually begin

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: and it tells you, “Oh my God, {{c1::how could I have done}}(我怎么可能做到的？) it? You’re so right again,” and brings back the first bug, and you can alternate between those.
original: and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,” and brings back the first bug, and you can alternate between those.
translation: 它会让你觉得，“天哪，我怎么能做到这个？你又说对了。”然后又把最初的那个 bug 找回来了，你可以在这些问题之间来回切换。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: {{c1::How is that possible}}(这怎么可能？)? I’m not sure, but it does suggest that something strange is going on.
original: How is that possible? I’m not sure, but it does suggest that something strange is going on.
translation: 这怎么可能呢？我不确定，但这确实暗示着，这里面正在发生一些奇怪的事情。
note:
phrases: How is that possible

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have {{c1::this kind of RL training}}(这种强化学习（RL）训练) for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
original: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
translation: 但实际上，当人们进行强化学习（RL）训练时，他们可不是随便做的。他们会先想：“好吧，针对这个任务，我们需要这种类型的RL训练；而针对那个任务，我们需要另一种类型的RL训练。”
note:
phrases: this kind of RL training
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0009_caedb9
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: abstraction_bridge

expression_transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just {{c1::add it to the training mix}}(把它加入到训练数据混合体中). The question is, well, what are those?
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those?
translation: 我听说，所有公司都有专门的团队，负责产出新的RL环境，然后把这些环境都加入到训练的混合体中。但问题是，这些环境到底是什么？
note:
phrases: add it to the training mix
title: Ilya-Sutskever

source_lang: en
target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Isn’t it {{c1::straight out of science fiction}}(简直像是科幻小说里走出来的)? Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels.
original: Isn’t it straight out of science fiction? Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels.
translation: 这听起来简直像是科幻小说里的情节，对吧？更让人觉得不可思议的是，这种“慢速起飞”的感觉，竟然如此正常。
note:

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I think the impact is going to be {{c1::felt very strongly}}(我感觉非常强烈). When do you expect that impact?
original: I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. When do you expect that impact?
translation: 我觉得它的影响会非常巨大的。你预计这种影响会在什么时候显现出来呢？

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: An example would be, let’s say you {{c1::use vibe coding}}(使用‘氛围编码’) to do something. You go to some place and then you {{c2::get a bug}}(遇到一个bug).
original: An example would be, let’s say you use vibe coding to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.
translation: 举个例子，假设你用一种凭感觉写代码的方式来做点什么。你跑到某个地方，然后就遇到了一个bug。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: AI is going to be {{c1::diffused through the economy}}(扩散到整个经济体). There’ll be very {{c2::strong economic forces}}(强大的经济力量) for this.
original: AI is going to be diffused through the economy. There’ll be very strong economic forces for this.
translation: 人工智能会渗透到整个经济领域。这背后会存在非常强大的经济驱动力。
note:
phrases: diffused through the economy | strong economic forces
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh
phrase_types: strong_collocation
transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.
expression_transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models {{c1::a little too single-minded}}(有点过于专一了) and {{c2::narrowly focused}}(范围太窄了), {{c3::a little bit too unaware}}(好像有点太脱节了，不够了解), even though it also {{c4::makes them aware}}(从而让人们意识到) in some other ways. Because of this, they can’t do basic things.
original: The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways. Because of this, they can’t do basic things.
translation: 更具推测性的解释是，也许强化学习（RL）训练让模型变得过于专注、过于狭隘，甚至有点缺乏全局意识，尽管它在其他方面也提高了模型的感知能力。正因为如此，它们反而无法完成一些基础性的任务。
note:
phrases: a little too single-minded | narrowly focused | a little bit too unaware | makes them aware
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever

original: They are doing so well. But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
translation: 它们的发展确实非常顺利。但从经济影响来看，似乎却落后了太多。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. {{c1::Let me go fix}}(让我去修一下) that.”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“你能帮我修一下这个bug吗？”模型就会说：“天哪，你说得太对了。我确实有个bug。让我去修一下。”
note:
